title: Hattie the Mouse's Custom Hat Shop
summary: Hattie the Mouse is busy in her workshop crafting custom hats for all her friends. From a little orange cardboard hat with sparkly mouse stickers for Jame to a sparkly silver hat for Silver Cat, Hattie meticulously measures and paints every creation. When she runs out of feathers for Jane’s special request, she must visit the supermarket to find the perfect rainbow feathers, glitter, and even a fishy pond to complete the most colorful hat of all.

highlightQuote.text: A fishy pond. Where you can put fishies and glitter in it. And you can do it after your nap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Silver Cat was playing at Hattie Mouse's house.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Hattie was playing in her workshop with Silver.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, she was making a hat for Silver Cat. For Jane. And Silver and Jane.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Hattie was making hats for Silver and for Jane.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And for Jame and for Jane.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Who was going to be first to get a hat? Silver, Jame or Jane?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Jame.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Jame was first. What kind of hat did he want to have? What kind of hat did he want?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, the stars are in the way.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Jame said, "I need a very tall hat."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But not a tall hat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said, "Not a tall hat." Jame said, "Oh okay, how about a short hat?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, a little hat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A little hat. And Hattie said, "What should it be made out of?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It should be made of paper and cardboard. Cardboard first and then you paint it and put sparkly bunnies on it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what color should we paint it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We should paint it orange.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Did you want sparkly hedgehog stickers? Is that what you said?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, sparkly bunny stickers. Actually, sparkly mouse stickers.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then who was next? Was it Silver Cat's turn?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mmm-hmm.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what kind of hat did Silver Cat want?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Silver Cat... she wants a medium size.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A medium-sized hat. And what should it be made out of?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nothing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie said, "We could make you an imaginary hat."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Not imaginary hat. A cardboard hat.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, the fishies are not glowing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, we just can't see them glow because it's not dark enough.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now it's Jane's turn.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was going to be a little hat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But there was one special thing about it. What was going to be special about it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know, you tell story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You must buy more at the supermarket. That's where they have lots of art supplies. Even tape and feathers.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: All kinds of stuff. Even like squishy toys.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And wiggly toys.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They also have music there. And they have feathers that you want. But which kind of feathers?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Rainbow feathers and the glitter area. And some glitter. And you need to buy some glitter. Some nice glitter. And a fishy pond too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And a what? What's that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A fishy pond. Where you can put fishies and glitter in it. And you can do it after your nap.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Dad, I think that the story's all done.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Silver Cat
characters.description: A cat who receives a medium-sized, sparkly silver cardboard hat from Hattie.

characters.name: Jame
characters.description: Hattie's friend who requests a little orange hat decorated with sparkly mouse stickers.

characters.name: Jane
characters.description: Dimetar’s matronly childhood companion is a kindhearted cat and daring ship’s pilot, a friend to Finn, Isabella, and Scratch Cat who navigates the expedition toward Mouse Castle and the road to Cadasori. Her cockpit is a warm sanctuary distinguished by a "love you" sticker and a cabin teeming with whiskered passengers, reflecting the same tenderness she first offered Silver. Though she maintains a bold pace, she is easily swayed by snacks at Seeker’s house or waylaid within the tangled depths of the dark forest. An adventurous spirit with whimsical desires, she currently dreams of a special little hat adorned with rainbow feathers, glitter, and a miniature fishy pond to crown her nautical ensemble.

places.name: Hattie's Workshop
places.description: Tucked within the cozy hollow of a weathered tree, Hattie Mouse’s dwelling serves as a busy, imaginative atelier and social hub where the air hums with the rhythmic snip of scissors. Here, amidst a cheerful clutter of measuring tapes, wet pigments, and scavenged shells, she crafts her signature starry hats from an eclectic mix of fur and feathers. The workshop is bathed in the earthy radiance of her fungal store—a brilliant collection of red, orange, and yellow mushrooms—illuminating the haunting indigo witch painting where Hattie first encountered the squeaking worm. It is in this storied corner, beneath the witch's gaze, that she recently unearthed a mysterious message hidden alongside a cache of sparkly green clover stickers.

places.name: The Supermarket
places.description: A bustling store that stocks everything from art supplies and feathers to squishy toys and music.
